activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of Physics (Meaning, branches and connection with other disciplines)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm different physical phenomena (natural and man-made) in their environment in relation to Physics
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through the use of ICT to come up with the meaning and branches of Physics
Field trip: Organise field trips to help students to explore the applications of Physics in real life experience
activities.assessment_criteria: Concept of Physics is clearly explained

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
activities.learning_activity: (b) Discuss the contribution of Physics to the development of modern society
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the contributions of Physics in the development of modern society
ICT-based learning: Guide the students through different Online sources and web-based information to explore contributions of Physics in the development of modern society
activities.assessment_criteria: Contributions of Physics to the development of modern society are clearly discussed

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
activities.learning_activity: (c) Explain concepts of physical quantities (fundamental and derived quantities) and their, SI units
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on measurable objects found in their environment
Jigsaw: Guide students through Jigsaw method to measure the size of various objects assign the SI Unit of the measured quantities
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of physical quantities are clearly explained

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
activities.learning_activity: (d) Describe concepts of linear motion (speed, velocity, acceleration, distance, and displacement)
suggested_methods: Circle the sage: Guide students through circle-the-sage to explore the concept of linear motion
Questions and answers: Facilitate the students through questions and answers on the real-life applications of linear motion
ICT based learning: Guide students through the use of interactive simulations to visualize the concept of linear motion
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of linear motion are clearly described

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
activities.learning_activity: (e) Explain the concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, work, power, energy
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure work, power and energy
ICT based learning: Guide students through the use of interactive simulations to visualize the concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, work, power, energy
Questions and answers: Use questions and answers to guide students explaining the principle related to force, density, pressure, power and energy
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, work, power and energy are explained clearly

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
activities.learning_activity: (f) Deduce the relationship between density, sinking and floating
suggested_methods: Experimentation: Guide the students to perform an experiment to deduce the relationship between density, sinking and floating
ICT-based learning: Guide student through the use of different online sources and web-based information to explore principles related to density, sinking and floating
activities.assessment_criteria: The relationship between density, sinking and floating is correctly deduced
suggested_resources: picture of floating and sinking objects and online resources

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
activities.learning_activity: (g) Describe the mechanical properties of matter in relation to force and energy
suggested_methods: Think-ink pair share: Guide the students through Think ink pair share to explore the mechanical properties of matter in relation to force and energy
Group discussion: Organize students in manageable groups to discuss different terms related to mechanical properties of matter
Experimentation: Guide the students through experiment to investigate the relationship between various mechanical properties of matter
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, power and energy are explained clearly
suggested_resources: glass jar with cork and rubber band

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate mastery of basic terminologies, measurements and symbols in Physics
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe various instruments used for measurement in Physics
suggested_methods: Circle-the-sage: Guide students through circle-the-sage strategy to describe various instruments used for measurements in Physics
Project work: Facilitate the students to describe various instruments used in Physics measurement
Virtual lab experiment: Facilitate the students through the use of Virtual lab to perform experiments to measure various objects
activities.assessment_criteria: Measuring instruments used in Physics measurements are correctly described
suggested_resources: vernier calliper and steel balls of varying diameters

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate mastery of basic terminologies, measurements and symbols in Physics
activities.learning_activity: (b) Relate measuring instruments to physical quantities
suggested_methods: Practical work and experimentation: Guide the students through practical work and experiment to measure different physical quantities by using appropriate instruments
activities.assessment_criteria: Precision and accuracy of measurements are clearly related

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Use mathematics to explain physical principles and phenomena
activities.learning_activity: (a) Use mathematical knowledge to describe relationship between various physical quantities (force, velocity, acceleration, density, pressure)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organize students in manageable groups through the use of mathematical knowledge to describe the relationship between various physical quantities
Problem solving: Guide the students to use physical principles to solve real life problems
activities.assessment_criteria: The mathematical knowledge is used to describe the relationship between various physical quantities
suggested_resources: Online resources and Physics reference books

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic experimental skills in Physics
activities.learning_activity: (a) Conduct experiments related to linear motion, density, force, pressure, work, energy, power and mechanical properties of matter
suggested_methods: Experimentation: Guide students through experiments to investigate the relationship between various parameters related to linear motion, density, force, pressure, work, energy, power and mechanical properties of matter

ICT based learning: Facilitate the students through the use of virtual lab, to investigate the principles related to linear motion, density, force, pressure, work, energy, power and mechanical properties of matter
activities.assessment_criteria: Experiments are properly conducted following the scientific method

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate mastery of data analysis, presentation and report writing in Physics
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the methods of analysing experimental data in physics (statistical analysis)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students in groups to investigate various methods used to analyse experimental data in physics (statistical analysis)
ICT based approach: Guide students through the use of ICT to investigate the best methods for analysing experimental data in Physics (statistical analysis)
activities.assessment_criteria: The methods of analysing experimental data are explained correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate mastery of data analysis, presentation and report writing in Physics
activities.learning_activity: (b) Use analytical methods to manipulate experimental data in physics
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Facilitate the students to brainstorm on the analytical methods used to manipulate experimental data in physics
ICT based approach: Guide students in utilizing interactive digital simulations to explore the hands-on activities for analytical methods to manipulate experimental data in physics
activities.assessment_criteria: Analytical methods to manipulate experimental data in physics are correctly performed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate mastery of data analysis, presentation and report writing in Physics
activities.learning_activity: (c) Use graphical method to present experimental results in physics
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Facilitate students through the use of graphical method to present experimental results in physics
Use of ICT facility: Guide students in utilizing interactive digital simulations and other software to present the experimental results using graphs
activities.assessment_criteria: The graphical method to present experimental results in physics is clearly used
suggested_resources: Physics reference books and different graph papers

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Evaluate and use information in Physics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Collect, describe and relate physical data
activities.learning_activity: (a) Collect and analyse data to explain various physical quantities (density, force, pressure)
suggested_methods: Cooperative learning strategy: Guide students to share their understanding on how to collect and analyse data to explain various physical parameters (density, force, pressure)
Project work: Facilitate students to do projects to collect, analyse data and explain various physical parameters (density, force, pressure)
activities.assessment_criteria: The data used to explain various physical parameters are collected and analysed appropriately

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Evaluate and use information in Physics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Collect, describe and relate physical data
activities.learning_activity: (b) Collect and analyse data to explain experimental observations related to linear motion, force, density, pressure and mechanical properties of matter
suggested_methods: ICT based approach: Guide students in utilizing interactive digital simulations to collect analyse data and explain experimental observations related to linear motion, force, density, pressure and mechanical properties of matter
Experimentation: Guide students in manageable groups to conduct experiments and analyse collected data
activities.assessment_criteria: The physical data to explain experimental observations related to given concepts are appropriately collected and analysed

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Evaluate and use information in Physics
activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Carry out a project in Physics
activities.learning_activity: (a) Develop prototype devices based on the concepts, theories, principles and laws gained from the field of linear motion, force, density, pressure and mechanical properties of matter
